Local History Getting Started
Winchester During COVID-19
It’s Easy! Present a photo ID and proof of your current address at any Circulation Desk. Massachusetts residency is required to get your library card and some services are restricted to Winchester residents only. Complete requirements are available in our policy manual.
You can access your online library account with your library card number and a password.
You’ll be able to see what you have checked out, renew your materials, manage requests, and pay fines even when the library is closed.
Have you received notification that your library card is expiring? If you have your library card number and know your PIN, you may be able to renew it online. If you are unable to renew online, please visit the library to renew your card.
Start from home Save time by filling out the registration form at home. You will be issued a temporary library card number, good for up to 6 months. Within that period, stop by with valid identification to be given your permanent card.
Children ages 4 and up with a parent or legal guardian can may get a library card.
The signature of a parent or guardian is not required for anyone from Grade 6 through age 17 years if the applicant can provide a photo ID and proof of address Otherwise the parent or legal guardian must provide proof of identification and address and sign the teen’s application. The teen must be present at the time the card is issued.

Winchester During COVID-19
The Library offers discounted passes to many local museums. Museum passes are generously provided by the Friends of the Library, the EnKa Society (Boston Children’s Museum, the New England Aquarium, and the Discovery Museum), and the Rotary Club of Winchester (Institute of Contemporary Art, Peabody Essex Museum, Trustees Go Pass, the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum, and Zoo New England).
Passes are available to Winchester residents only.
A household may reserve passes for two museums per week, defined as Monday-Sunday.
Some passes need to be returned and these are due by 9:30 AM the day after use. Late passes are subject to a fine of $5 per day.
See the details on pass pickup and return.
See also instructions for purchasing discounted tickets with your pass.
Please call the Reference desk at 781-721-7171 x320.
